items.title: EmoEUS: Uncertainty Supervision for Multimodal Emotion Recognition in Conversation
items.abstract: Multimodal emotion recognition in conversation (MERC) can leverage multimodal and contextual cues to boost recognition performance. However, existing fusion approaches in MERC often ignore modality-specific uncertainty across utterances caused by conflicting cues, varying noise, and missing modality-specific signals. We propose EmoEUS, an explicit uncertainty supervision framework for MERC. EmoEUS performs uncertainty-aware multimodal fusion by dynamically weighting modalities using learned variance estimates. We also introduce an explicitly supervised loss that aligns each utterance's predicted variance with the distance between the utterance's distributional representation and its emotion- and modality-specific cluster center. Experiments on IEMOCAP and MELD show that EmoEUS consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
items.llm_evidence_en: Uncertainty-aware multimodal fusion using learned variance estimates
items.llm_evidence_cn: 基于学习方差估计的不确定性感知多模态融合
items.llm_evidence: 基于学习方差估计的不确定性感知多模态融合
items.canonical_evidence: 基于学习方差估计的不确定性感知多模态融合
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes EmoEUS for multimodal emotion recognition in conversation, which performs uncertainty-aware fusion by dynamically weighting modalities with learned variance estimates and aligning distributional representations, improving recognition accuracy.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 对话多模态情感识别中现有融合方法忽略模态间冲突、噪声和缺失带来的不确定性。本文提出EmoEUS框架，通过学习方差估计动态加权模态进行不确定性感知融合，并设计显式监督损失将预测方差与分布表示对齐。在多个公开数据集上实验表明，该方法显著提升了识别准确率，尤其在处理冲突模态时表现突出，为多模态融合中的不确定性建模提供了有效方案。
items.llm_tldr: 对话多模态情感识别中现有融合方法忽略模态间冲突、噪声和缺失带来的不确定性。本文提出EmoEUS框架，通过学习方差估计动态加权模态进行不确定性感知融合，并设计显式监督损失将预测方差与分布表示对齐。在多个公开数据集上实验表明，该方法显著提升了识别准确率，尤其在处理冲突模态时表现突出，为多模态融合中的不确定性建模提供了有效方案。

items.title: Not All Patches are Equal: Sampling Matters for Visible-Infrared Pre-Training
items.abstract: Visible-infrared (VIS-IR) alignment is a key pre-training task for robust multi-sensor perception. Most existing methods use uniform patch-wise contrastive learning, but this can be unreliable in VIS-IR data because imaging-physics differences make some spatially paired regions inherently less comparable, and aligning them with equal strength hinders representation learning and downstream transfer. In this paper, we revisit VIS-IR pre-training from a sampling perspective and propose Importance-Aware Sampling (IAS), which adjusts training emphasis based on patch reliability. Specifically, IAS (i) derives patch weights from infrared structural cues and uses them to reweight the contrastive objective; (ii) learns a soft importance mask with a lightweight sampler, optionally warm-started from the hand-crafted prior; and (iii) employs a patch curriculum learning strategy that gradually expands from high-reliability regions to harder patches. It is worth noting that IAS is plug-and-play and works with both patch-/correlation-level alignment (e.g., UNIV-style) and image-level contrastive baselines (e.g., ImageBind-style). Extensive experiments on multiple VIS-IR benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvements over strong baselines, including for IR semantic segmentation, IR object detection and VIS semantic segmentation and cross-modal retrieval task. Code will be released on https://github.com/KlayMa527/IAS.
items.llm_evidence_en: importance-aware sampling for visible-infrared alignment, a cross-modal alignment strategy
items.llm_evidence_cn: 针对可见光-红外对齐的重要性感知采样，一种跨模态对齐策略
items.llm_evidence: 针对可见光-红外对齐的重要性感知采样，一种跨模态对齐策略

items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Importance-Aware Sampling (IAS) for VIS-IR pre-training, adjusting training emphasis based on patch reliability to improve cross-modal alignment.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 可见光-红外对齐中统一对比学习因成像差异不可靠。本文提出重要性感知采样，从红外结构线索导出补丁权重，并学习软重要性掩膜以重新加权对比目标，从而提升跨模态表示质量和下游任务性能。
items.llm_tldr: 可见光-红外对齐中统一对比学习因成像差异不可靠。本文提出重要性感知采样，从红外结构线索导出补丁权重，并学习软重要性掩膜以重新加权对比目标，从而提升跨模态表示质量和下游任务性能。

items.title: Test-Time Training for Modality Order Consistency in Vision-Language Models
items.abstract: We find that vision-language models are sensitive to a specific semantically irrelevant change: the order in which the image and question are presented. Across three models and three benchmarks, image first prompting consistently outperforms question-first prompting, revealing a repeatable modality order failure. We use this gap to design an order-consistent test-time training method. Our method substantially closes the modality-order gap across all evaluated settings. Surprisingly, it also yields consistent improvements in the stronger image-first branch over the baseline, hence bootstrapping both orderings toward mutual consistency. Activation patching localizes the ordering failure to a narrow mid-network region where representations diverge sharply between prompt orders. We find that the test-time training method repairs this misalignment across layers. Together, our results identify modality-order sensitivity as a circuit-level failure in VLMs and demonstrate that simple, asymmetric test-time adaptation can effectively mitigate it and even improve performance over the baseline.
items.llm_evidence_en: cross-modal alignment through test-time training for modality order consistency
items.llm_evidence_cn: 通过测试时训练实现模态顺序一致性，跨模态对齐
items.llm_evidence: 通过测试时训练实现模态顺序一致性，跨模态对齐
items.canonical_evidence: 通过测试时训练实现模态顺序一致性，跨模态对齐
items.llm_tldr_en: Identifies modality order sensitivity in VLMs and proposes test-time training to align representations across image-first and question-first orders.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉语言模型对图像和问题输入的顺序敏感，图像优先始终优于问题优先。本文将此差距用于设计顺序一致的测试时训练方法，通过微调小型适配器缩小模态顺序差距，并同时提升图像优先分支的性能。激活修补定位到中层网络区域，揭示了表示分叉的机制。该方法有效实现了跨模态顺序对齐。
items.llm_tldr: 视觉语言模型对图像和问题输入的顺序敏感，图像优先始终优于问题优先。本文将此差距用于设计顺序一致的测试时训练方法，通过微调小型适配器缩小模态顺序差距，并同时提升图像优先分支的性能。激活修补定位到中层网络区域，揭示了表示分叉的机制。该方法有效实现了跨模态顺序对齐。

items.title: GATE-3D: Geometry-Aware Test-time Adaptive Reranking for Open-Set 3D Shape Retrieval
items.abstract: Large pretrained vision models have substantially improved appearance-based 3D shape retrieval, but they still confuse shapes that look similar while differing in geometry. Although geometry-aware features can reduce these errors, naive fusion of geometry and appearance may hurt retrieval when the two modalities are already well aligned. We propose GATE-3D, a lightweight query-adaptive reranking method that incorporates geometry without retraining the retrieval backbone. For each query, GATE-3D predicts how much a geometry-aware score should adjust the appearance-based ranking using features that capture disagreement between the two modalities. This selective design lets geometry contribute where it helps and stay silent where it would hurt. Experiments on three open-set 3D retrieval benchmarks show that GATE-3D improves over appearance-only retrieval and is more robust than always-on fusion. On the primary benchmark, it improves mAP@10 by 2.00 points over appearance-only retrieval (p=0.041); it also improves leave-one-category-out generalization and reduces geometric false positives by 10.8%. GATE-3D achieves competitive zero-shot results against DAC-based baselines. We further find that simple linear routing is more effective than a small MLP in the low-data regime, suggesting that cross-modal disagreement features matter more than model capacity for adaptive routing.
items.llm_evidence_en: geometry-aware fusion with appearance for 3D shape retrieval, adaptive multimodal fusion
items.llm_evidence_cn: 几何与外观的适应性融合，用于3D形状检索的多模态融合
items.llm_evidence: 几何与外观的适应性融合，用于3D形状检索的多模态融合
items.canonical_evidence: 几何与外观的适应性融合，用于3D形状检索的多模态融合
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes query-adaptive reranking that fuses geometry and appearance features selectively, improving open-set 3D shape retrieval.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 基于外观的3D形状检索容易混淆几何不同但外观相似的形状。本文提出GATE-3D轻量级重排序方法，预测几何感知分数应调整外观排序的程度，只在几何有益时进行融合。该方法无需重训练骨干网络，在多个数据集上提升了开放集检索精度，体现了几何与外观的智能多模态融合。
items.llm_tldr: 基于外观的3D形状检索容易混淆几何不同但外观相似的形状。本文提出GATE-3D轻量级重排序方法，预测几何感知分数应调整外观排序的程度，只在几何有益时进行融合。该方法无需重训练骨干网络，在多个数据集上提升了开放集检索精度，体现了几何与外观的智能多模态融合。

items.title: PolySim: Deterministic Polynomial Surrogates for Cross-Modal Retrieval on CiM
items.abstract: Cross-modal retrieval on edge devices benefits from probabilistic embeddings that capture semantic uncertainty, but deploying them on compute-in-memory (CiM) hardware remains an open problem. The core difficulty is a sampling gap: probabilistic methods such as PCME rely on Monte Carlo sampling and nonlinear distance evaluation at inference, which are fundamentally incompatible with CiM crossbar arrays that support only deterministic, single-step matrix-vector multiplication. Few existing probabilistic retrieval methods can be executed on a conventional crossbar. To bridge this gap, we propose PolySim, a framework that reformulates probabilistic retrieval into a fully deterministic pipeline. PolySim approximates each Gaussian embedding dimension using low-order polynomial bases and computes similarity via a learnable order-bilinear kernel, eliminating stochastic sampling while preserving distributional information. In experiments on six benchmarks spanning video, image, and audio retrieval, PolySim improves R@1 over deterministic baselines by up to 10.3\% and matches or exceeds PCME, while reducing inference to a single crossbar-compatible matrix-vector multiplication. CrossSim evaluation under realistic device non-idealities confirms robust deployment on conventional crossbar arrays. To the best of our knowledge, PolySim is the first method to enable probabilistic cross-modal retrieval on CiM hardware.
items.llm_evidence_en: cross-modal retrieval with deterministic polynomial surrogates
items.llm_evidence_cn: 跨模态检索的确定性多项式代理
items.llm_evidence: 跨模态检索的确定性多项式代理
items.canonical_evidence: 跨模态检索的确定性多项式代理
items.llm_tldr_en: PolySim reformulates probabilistic cross-modal retrieval into a deterministic pipeline using polynomial surrogates, enabling deployment on compute-in-memory hardware.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 跨模态检索在边缘设备上部署受限于概率方法的高斯采样与非线性距离计算。PolySim通过多项式代理将概率检索转化为完全确定性的流水线，首次实现了在存内计算硬件上的高效跨模态检索，解决了采样鸿沟问题。
items.llm_tldr: 跨模态检索在边缘设备上部署受限于概率方法的高斯采样与非线性距离计算。PolySim通过多项式代理将概率检索转化为完全确定性的流水线，首次实现了在存内计算硬件上的高效跨模态检索，解决了采样鸿沟问题。

items.title: Dual-Edged Homogeneous-Modality Similarity: Towards Visible-Infrared Modality-Incomplete Person Re-Identification with Modality Adaptive Matching
items.abstract: Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification (VI-ReID) operates under a closed-world assumption, where queries and galleries are from heterogeneous modalities. However, in open-world scenarios, both sets are likely to contain homogeneous and heterogeneous modality images. A query may consist of visible-only, infrared-only, or mixed-modality images, while galleries present multi-modal images over long-term collection. Under these conditions, VI-ReID methods, built on a heterogeneous-modality retrieval paradigm, suffer from three trustworthiness challenges: matching conflicts due to high homogeneous-modality similarity, interference from modality uncertainty, and robustness degradation induced by unknown modality combinations. They fail to meet the requirements of trustworthy visual recognition in reliability, consistency, and dynamic adaptability. To address these challenges, we formalize the Visible-Infrared Modality-Incomplete Re-Identification (VIMI-ReID) task. We reorganize existing datasets to construct the SYSU-VIMI and RegDB-VIMI benchmarks. The unpredictable modality combinations and inherent similarity of homogeneous-modality samples in VIMI-ReID cause a significant performance drop in existing VI-ReID methods. We propose the Modality Adaptive Matching Transformer (MAMT). It employs a Divergence Transformer Module (DTM) and a Shared Transformer Module (STM) to extract modality-specific and modality-shared features, respectively. Guided by a divergence loss, the DTM enriches modality-specific features with modality-style information to enhance discriminability within the same modality. A Modality Adaptive Matching Module (MAM) dynamically fuses features according to the query-gallery modality relationship, enabling stable matching under arbitrary and uncertain modality conditions. Extensive experiments on the VIMI benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness and adaptability of MAMT.
items.llm_evidence_en: multimodal representation learning under modality-incomplete data
items.llm_evidence_cn: 模态不完整数据下的多模态表示学习

items.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses visible-infrared person re-identification with modality-incomplete data, proposing modality adaptive matching to handle heterogeneous and homogeneous queries.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 开放世界场景下可见光-红外行人重识别面临查询模态不完整问题。本文提出模态自适应匹配方法，同时处理同质和异质模态图像，解决了因同质模态高相似度导致的匹配冲突和不确定性干扰，提升了鲁棒性和可信度。
items.llm_tldr: 开放世界场景下可见光-红外行人重识别面临查询模态不完整问题。本文提出模态自适应匹配方法，同时处理同质和异质模态图像，解决了因同质模态高相似度导致的匹配冲突和不确定性干扰，提升了鲁棒性和可信度。
